---
title: "Theorem 3.2 — OLS normal equations and orthogonality of regressors and residuals"
lean_name: "HansenEconometrics.normal_equations"
chapter: 3
---

## Textbook statement

Hansen Theorem 3.2 states: when $X^\top X$ is invertible, the OLS estimator
$$\hat\beta = (X^\top X)^{-1} X^\top y$$
satisfies the **normal equations**
$$X^\top (y - X\hat\beta) = 0,$$
equivalently $X^\top \hat e = 0$ where $\hat e = y - X\hat\beta$ is the residual
vector. So the regressors are orthogonal to the OLS residuals.

## Assumptions

- Design matrix $X \in \mathbb{R}^{n\times k}$.
- $X^\top X$ is invertible (equivalently, $X$ has full column rank, $k \le n$).
- $y \in \mathbb{R}^n$ is the response vector. No distributional assumption is
  needed — Theorem 3.2 is purely linear-algebraic.

## Lean statement

The corresponding Lean objects all live in
`HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean`:

- [`olsBeta`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean#L20)
  (definition, line 20) — the closed-form OLS coefficient
  $(X^\top X)^{-1} X^\top y$, defined under `[Invertible (Xᵀ * X)]`.
- [`fitted`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean#L38)
  (definition, line 38) — the fitted vector $X\hat\beta$.
- [`residual`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean#L42)
  (definition, line 42) — the residual vector $y - X\hat\beta$.
- [`normal_equations`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean#L46)
  (theorem, **line 46**) — the headline result:
  `Xᵀ *ᵥ residual X y = 0`.
- [`regressors_orthogonal_to_residual`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean#L61)
  (theorem, line 61) — same statement, packaged under the orthogonality reading.
- [`olsBeta_eq_of_normal_equations`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean#L67)
  (theorem, line 67) — uniqueness: any `b` satisfying the normal equations
  equals `olsBeta`.

The headline Lean theorem reads:

```lean
theorem normal_equations
    (X : Matrix n k ℝ) (y : n → ℝ) [Invertible (Xᵀ * X)] :
    Xᵀ *ᵥ residual X y = 0
```

## Translation notes

- Hansen writes $X' \hat e = 0$ in matrix-of-real-numbers notation. Lean's
  `Xᵀ *ᵥ residual X y = 0` is the same statement under Mathlib's
  `Matrix.mulVec` / transpose conventions.
- Lean parameterizes by typeclasses `[Fintype n] [Fintype k] [DecidableEq k]`
  and an `[Invertible (Xᵀ * X)]` instance instead of a side condition. This is
  the same hypothesis as Hansen's "$X^\top X$ is invertible," packaged for
  elaboration.
- The pair `olsBeta` (using `⅟`) and `olsBetaStar` (using `Matrix.nonsingInv`)
  coexist because Chapter 7's stochastic story needs the total version. They
  agree under invertibility
  ([`olsBetaStar_eq_olsBeta`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean#L31)
  at line 31).

## Proof sketch

Substitute the closed form $\hat\beta = (X^\top X)^{-1} X^\top y$ into
$X^\top (y - X\hat\beta)$:
$$X^\top y - X^\top X (X^\top X)^{-1} X^\top y = X^\top y - X^\top y = 0.$$
That's the whole proof — the normal equations are immediate from the
definition of $\hat\beta$.

Uniqueness (`olsBeta_eq_of_normal_equations`) goes the other direction: from
$X^\top(y - Xb) = 0$, multiply through by $(X^\top X)^{-1}$ to recover
$b = (X^\top X)^{-1} X^\top y = \hat\beta$.

## Lean proof structure

The Lean proof of
[`normal_equations`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean#L46)
mirrors the textbook proof in three short steps:

- Unfold `residual`, `fitted`, and `olsBeta`, then split the `mulVec` over
  subtraction with [`Matrix.mulVec_sub`](https://leanprover-community.github.io/mathlib4_docs/Mathlib/Data/Matrix/Mul.html).
- Use [`Matrix.mulVec_mulVec`](https://leanprover-community.github.io/mathlib4_docs/Mathlib/Data/Matrix/Mul.html)
  to commute the two `*ᵥ` applications into a single matrix product, then
  reassociate via [`Matrix.mul_assoc`](https://leanprover-community.github.io/mathlib4_docs/Mathlib/Data/Matrix/Mul.html)
  so that $X^\top X$ sits next to its inverse.
- Collapse $(X^\top X) \cdot ⅟(X^\top X)$ to $1$ using
  [`mul_invOf_self`](https://leanprover-community.github.io/mathlib4_docs/Mathlib/Algebra/GroupWithZero/Invertible.html),
  leaving $X^\top y - X^\top y$, which `simp` reduces to $0$.

For uniqueness,
[`olsBeta_eq_of_normal_equations`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean#L67)
(line 67) takes the hypothesis $X^\top(y - Xb) = 0$, rewrites it as
$X^\top y = (X^\top X) b$, and multiplies both sides by $⅟(X^\top X)$ to
identify $b$ with `olsBeta X y`.

## Downstream uses

`normal_equations` is one of the most reused lemmas in the algebra of OLS.
Direct consumers in this repo include:

- [`residual_sum_zero_of_one_mem_colspan`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean#L98)
  (Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean:98) — when $\mathbf{1} \in \mathrm{col}(X)$,
  residuals sum to zero (Hansen equation 3.17).
- [`regressors_transpose_mul_annihilator`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3FWL.lean#L37)
  (Chapter3FWL.lean:37) — $X^\top M = 0$, used throughout FWL.
- [`fitted_dot_residual`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L217)
  (Chapter3Projections.lean:217) — fitted values are orthogonal to residuals.
- The Frisch–Waugh–Lovell theorems of Hansen Theorem 3.5 consume this
  transitively through the annihilator identity.
